source.title: Temu App Store Rating and International Review Data (2025-2026)

extractions.claim: Temu iOS App Store rating: 4.67/5.0 with 2.07M ratings (US, March 2026)
extractions.evidence: averageUserRating: 4.67302, userRatingCount: 2,069,130. Version 4.39.0. Primary genre: Shopping.

extractions.claim: Temu app downloads: 1B+ cumulative by late 2025, top-3 most downloaded shopping app in US App Store (March 2026)
extractions.evidence: Over 1 billion cumulative downloads by late 2025. Temu has returned to the top of the App Store charts, ranking third in the most-downloaded shopping apps after resuming ad spend.

extractions.claim: Stark divergence: Temu iOS rating 4.67/5 vs Trustpilot 2.0-2.5/5 vs Sitejabber 2.0/5. App store reviews skew positive (prompted after purchase), complaint sites skew negative (self-selection).
extractions.evidence: App Store: 4.67/5 (2.07M ratings). Trustpilot: 2.0-2.5/5 (~49K reviews). Sitejabber: 2.0/5 (1,760 reviews). BBB: 2.29/5 (1,805 reviews). hellopeter: 2.8 TrustIndex.

verification.verifier_notes: Cross-platform comparison. Divergence is consistent with known bias patterns: app store ratings prompted after positive experiences; Trustpilot/Sitejabber self-selected complaint-driven.

extractions.claim: Pinduoduo iOS App Store rating in China: 2.9/5 with 3.38M ratings — notably lower than Temu's US rating (4.67/5)
extractions.evidence: Pinduoduo CN App Store: 2.9/5 (3,384,490 ratings). Pinduoduo US: 2.4/5 (7,017 ratings). Temu US: 4.67/5 (2,069,130 ratings).

verification.verifier_notes: iTunes Lookup API. PDD's domestic app has significantly more ratings but lower score, likely reflecting mature user base with higher complaint rates.

extractions.claim: Temu regulatory actions (2025-2026): Netherlands banned ads targeting children (Mar 2025), Belgium reprimanded for biometric data harvesting (Jan 2026), FTC $2M fine resolved (Sep 2025), Texas AG sued for 'spyware' (Feb 2026)
extractions.evidence: Netherlands banned targeted ads to children under 16 (March 2025). Belgium DPA formal reprimand for unlawful biometric data via AR 'Try-On' (January 2026). Whaleco Inc resolved FTC INFORM Act issues with $2M fine (September 2025). NCC Group audit: Medium Risk rating, only 12% 2FA adoption.

verification.verifier_notes: Aggregated from multiple regulatory and news sources
